contentBlocks.original:
ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान् मृत्योर्मुक्षीय माऽमृतात्
contentBlocks.transliteration:
oṃ tryambakaṃ yajāmahe sugandhiṃ puṣṭivardhanam
urvārukamiva bandhanān mṛtyormukṣīya mā'mṛtāt
contentBlocks.translation: We bow to the three-eyed one (Lord Shiva), the sustainer of all, who nourishes and nurtures all beings. Like the ripe cucumber that effortlessly releases from the vine, may he liberate us from the bondage of worldly attachments and the cycle of birth and death, bestowing us with the nectar of immortality.
contentBlocks.meanings: ॐ (oṃ) – sacred syllable; त्र्यम्बकं (tryambakaṃ) – three-eyed one (tri:three | ambaka:eye); यजामहे (yajāmahe) – we worship; सुगन्धिं (sugandhiṃ) – fragrant (su:good | gandhi:smell); पुष्टिवर्धनम् (puṣṭivardhanam) – nourisher (puṣṭi:nourishment | vardhana:increaser); उर्वारुकमिव (urvārukamiva) – like a cucumber (urvāruka:cucumber | iva:like); बन्धनान् (bandhanān) – from bondage; मृत्योः (mṛtyoḥ) – from death; मुक्षीय (mukṣīya) – may we be liberated; मा (mā) – not; अमृतात् (amṛtāt) – from immortality;
